kv.allocator.lease_io_overload_threshold.description: a store will not receive new leases when its IO overload score is above this value and `kv.allocator.io_overload_threshold` is `shed` or `block_transfer_to`
kv.allocator.lease_io_overload_threshold.name: kv.allocator.lease_io_overload_threshold

kv.allocator.lease_io_overload_threshold.description: the level of enforcement on lease transfers when a candidate store has anio overload score exceeding `kv.allocator.io_overload_threshold_lease` and above the average of comparable allocation candidates:`ignore` disable enforcement, `block_transfer_to` a store will receive no new leases but won't lose existing leases,`shed`: a store will receive no new leases and shed existing leases to non io-overloaded stores, this is a superset of block_transfer_to [ignore = 0, block_transfer_to = 1, shed = 3]
kv.allocator.lease_io_overload_threshold.name: kv.allocator.lease_io_overload_threshold_enforcement

kv.allocator.lease_rebalance_threshold.description: minimum fraction away from the mean a store's lease count can be before it is considered for lease-transfers
kv.allocator.lease_rebalance_threshold.name: kv.allocator.lease_rebalance_threshold

kv.allocator.lease_rebalancing_aggressiv.description: set greater than 1.0 to rebalance leases toward load more aggressively, or between 0 and 1.0 to be more conservative about rebalancing leases
kv.allocator.lease_rebalancing_aggressiv.name: kv.allocator.lease_rebalancing_aggressiveness

kv.allocator.lease_shed_io_overload_thre.description: a store will shed its leases and receive no new leases when its IO overload score is above this value and `kv.allocator.lease_io_overload_threshold_enforcement` is `shed`
kv.allocator.lease_shed_io_overload_thre.name: kv.allocator.lease_shed_io_overload_threshold

kv.allocator.load_based_lease_rebalancin.description: set to enable rebalancing of range leases based on load and latency
kv.allocator.load_based_lease_rebalancin.name: kv.allocator.load_based_lease_rebalancing.enabled

kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing.description: whether to rebalance based on the distribution of load across stores [off = 0, leases = 1, leases and replicas = 2]
kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing.name: kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing

kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing.obje.description: what objective does the cluster use to rebalance; if set to `qps` the cluster will attempt to balance qps among stores, if set to `cpu` the cluster will attempt to balance cpu usage among stores [qps = 0, cpu = 1]
kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing.obje.name: kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing.objective

kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing_inte.description: the rough interval at which each store will check for load-based lease / replica rebalancing opportunities
kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing_inte.name: kv.allocator.load_based_rebalancing_interval

kv.allocator.max_disk_utilization_thresh.description: maximum disk utilization before a store will never be used as a rebalance or allocation target and will actively have replicas moved off of it; this should be set higher than `kv.allocator.rebalance_to_max_disk_utilization_threshold`
kv.allocator.max_disk_utilization_thresh.name: kv.allocator.max_disk_utilization_threshold

kv.allocator.min_io_overload_lease_shed_.description: controls how frequently all leases can be shed from a node due to the node becoming IO overloaded
kv.allocator.min_io_overload_lease_shed_.name: kv.allocator.min_io_overload_lease_shed_interval

kv.allocator.min_lease_transfer_interval.description: controls how frequently leases can be transferred for rebalancing. It does not prevent transferring leases in order to allow a replica to be removed from a range.
kv.allocator.min_lease_transfer_interval.name: kv.allocator.min_lease_transfer_interval

kv.allocator.min_qps_difference_for_tran.description: the minimum qps difference that must exist between any two stores for the allocator to allow a lease or replica transfer between them
kv.allocator.min_qps_difference_for_tran.name: kv.allocator.min_qps_difference_for_transfers

kv.allocator.qps_rebalance_threshold.description: minimum fraction away from the mean a store's QPS (such as queries per second) can be before it is considered overfull or underfull
kv.allocator.qps_rebalance_threshold.name: kv.allocator.qps_rebalance_threshold

kv.allocator.range_rebalance_threshold.description: minimum fraction away from the mean a store's range count can be before it is considered overfull or underfull
kv.allocator.range_rebalance_threshold.name: kv.allocator.range_rebalance_threshold

kv.allocator.rebalance_to_max_disk_utili.description: maximum disk utilization before a store will never be used as a rebalance target; this should be set lower than `kv.allocator.max_disk_utilization_threshold`
kv.allocator.rebalance_to_max_disk_utili.name: kv.allocator.rebalance_to_max_disk_utilization_threshold

kv.allocator.recovery_store_selector.description: if set to 'good', the allocator may recover replicas to any valid store, if set to 'best' it will pick one of the most ideal stores
kv.allocator.recovery_store_selector.name: kv.allocator.recovery_store_selector

kv.allocator.replica_io_overload_thresho.description: the maximum store io overload before the enforcement defined by `kv.allocator.io_overload_threshold_enforce` is taken on a store for allocation decisions
kv.allocator.replica_io_overload_thresho.name: kv.allocator.replica_io_overload_threshold

kv.allocator.replica_io_overload_thresho.description: the level of enforcement when a candidate store has an io overload score  exceeding `kv.allocator.io_overload_threshold` and above the average of comparable allocation candidates:`block_none` will exclude no candidate stores, `block_none_log` will exclude no candidates but log an event, `block_rebalance_to` will exclude candidates stores from being targets of rebalance actions, `block_all` will exclude candidate stores from being targets of both allocation and rebalancing [ignore = 0, block_rebalance_to = 1, block_all = 2]
kv.allocator.replica_io_overload_thresho.name: kv.allocator.replica_io_overload_threshold_enforcement

kv.allocator.store_cpu_rebalance_thresho.description: minimum fraction away from the mean a store's cpu usage can be before it is considered overfull or underfull
kv.allocator.store_cpu_rebalance_thresho.name: kv.allocator.store_cpu_rebalance_threshold

kv.bulk_ingest.pk_buffer_size.name: kv.bulk_ingest.pk_buffer_size

kv.bulk_io_write.concurrent_addsstable_a.name: kv.bulk_io_write.concurrent_addsstable_as_writes_requests

kv.bulk_io_write.max_rate.name: kv.bulk_io_write.max_rate

kv.bulk_io_write.min_capacity_remaining_.description: remaining store capacity fraction below which bulk ingestion requests are rejected
kv.bulk_io_write.min_capacity_remaining_.name: kv.bulk_io_write.min_capacity_remaining_fraction

kv.bulk_io_write.restore_node_concurrenc.description: the number of workers processing a restore per job per node; maximum 32

kv.bulk_io_write.small_write_size.name: kv.bulk_io_write.small_write_size

kv.closed_timestamp.propagation_slack.description: a conservative estimate of the amount of time expect for closed timestamps to propagate from a leaseholder to followers. This is taken into account by follower_read_timestamp().
kv.closed_timestamp.propagation_slack.name: kv.closed_timestamp.propagation_slack

sql.zone_configs.max_replicas_per_region.name: sql.zone_configs.max_replicas_per_region

sqladmission.elastic_cpu.duration_per_lo.description: controls how many CPU tokens are allotted for handling responses for internally submitted low priority reads
sqladmission.elastic_cpu.duration_per_lo.name: sqladmission.elastic_cpu.duration_per_low_pri_read_response

sqladmission.low_pri_read_response_elast.description: determines whether the sql portion of internally submitted reads integrate with elastic CPU controller
sqladmission.low_pri_read_response_elast.name: sqladmission.low_pri_read_response_elastic_control.enabled

storage.block_load.node_max_active.description: maximum number of outstanding sstable block reads per host
storage.block_load.node_max_active.name: storage.block_load.node_max_active

storage.columnar_blocks.enabled.description: set to true to enable columnar-blocks to store KVs in a columnar format
storage.columnar_blocks.enabled.name: storage.columnar_blocks.enabled

storage.compaction_concurrency.description: the baseline number of concurrent compactions
storage.compaction_concurrency.name: storage.compaction_concurrency

storage.delete_compaction_excise.enabled.description: set to false to direct Pebble to not partially excise sstables in delete-only compactions
storage.delete_compaction_excise.enabled.name: storage.delete_compaction_excise.enabled

storage.ingest_as_flushable.enabled.description: set to true to enable lazy ingestion of sstables
storage.ingest_as_flushable.enabled.name: storage.ingest_as_flushable.enabled

storage.ingest_split.enabled.description: set to false to disable ingest-time splitting that lowers write-amplification
storage.ingest_split.enabled.name: storage.ingest_split.enabled

storage.ingestion.value_blocks.enabled.description: set to true to enable writing of value blocks in ingestion sstables
storage.ingestion.value_blocks.enabled.name: storage.ingestion.value_blocks.enabled

storage.max_compaction_concurrency.description: the maximum number of concurrent compactions (0 = default); the default value is min(3,numCPUs-1) or what the COCKROACH_CONCURRENT_COMPACTIONS env var specifies; the env var also takes precedence over the cluster setting when the latter is lower
storage.max_compaction_concurrency.name: storage.max_compaction_concurrency

storage.max_download_compaction_concurre.description: the maximum number of concurrent download compactions
storage.max_download_compaction_concurre.name: storage.max_download_compaction_concurrency

storage.max_sync_duration.description: maximum duration for disk operations; any operations that take longer than this setting trigger a warning log entry or process crash
storage.max_sync_duration.name: storage.max_sync_duration

storage.max_sync_duration.fatal.enabled.description: if true, fatal the process when a disk operation exceeds storage.max_sync_duration
storage.max_sync_duration.fatal.enabled.name: storage.max_sync_duration.fatal.enabled

storage.mvcc.max_intents_per_error.description: maximum number of locks returned in errors during evaluation
storage.mvcc.max_intents_per_error.name: storage.mvcc.max_conflicts_per_lock_conflict_error

storage.mvcc.target_intent_bytes_per_err.description: maximum total lock size returned in errors during evaluation
storage.mvcc.target_intent_bytes_per_err.name: storage.mvcc.target_bytes_per_lock_conflict_error

storage.readahead_mode.informed.description: the readahead mode for operations which are known to read through large chunks of data; sys-readahead performs explicit prefetching via the readahead syscall; fadv-sequential lets the OS perform prefetching via fadvise(FADV_SEQUENTIAL) [off = 0, sys-readahead = 1, fadv-sequential = 2]
storage.readahead_mode.informed.name: storage.readahead_mode.informed

storage.readahead_mode.speculative.description: the readahead mode that is used automatically when sequential reads are detected; sys-readahead performs explicit prefetching via the readahead syscall; fadv-sequential starts with explicit prefetching via the readahead syscall then automatically switches to OS-driven prefetching via fadvise(FADV_SEQUENTIAL) [off = 0, sys-readahead = 1, fadv-sequential = 2]
storage.readahead_mode.speculative.name: storage.readahead_mode.speculative

storage.sstable.compression_algorithm.description: determines the compression algorithm to use when compressing sstable data blocks for use in a Pebble store (balanced,good are experimental); [snappy = 1, zstd = 2, none = 3, minlz = 4, fastest = 5, balanced = 6, good = 7]
storage.sstable.compression_algorithm.name: storage.sstable.compression_algorithm

storage.sstable.compression_algorithm_ba.description: determines the compression algorithm to use when compressing sstable data blocks for backup row data storage (fast,balanced,good are experimental); [snappy = 1, zstd = 2, none = 3, minlz = 4, fastest = 5, fast = 6, balanced = 7, good = 8]
storage.sstable.compression_algorithm_ba.name: storage.sstable.compression_algorithm_backup_storage

storage.sstable.compression_algorithm_ba.description: determines the compression algorithm to use when compressing sstable data blocks for backup transport (fast,balanced,good are experimental); [snappy = 1, zstd = 2, none = 3, minlz = 4, fastest = 5, fast = 6, balanced = 7, good = 8]
storage.sstable.compression_algorithm_ba.name: storage.sstable.compression_algorithm_backup_transport

storage.transaction.local_timestamps.ena.description: if enabled, MVCC keys will be written with local timestamps
storage.transaction.local_timestamps.ena.name: storage.transaction.local_timestamps.enabled

storage.value_separation.compaction_garb.description: the max garbage threshold configures the percentage of unreferenced value bytes that trigger blob-file rewrite compactions; 100 disables these compactions
storage.value_separation.compaction_garb.name: storage.value_separation.compaction_garbage_threshold

storage.value_separation.enabled.description: (experimental) whether or not values may be separated into blob files; requires columnar blocks to be enabled
storage.value_separation.enabled.name: storage.value_separation.enabled

storage.value_separation.max_reference_d.description: the max reference depth bounds the number of unique, overlapping blob files referenced within a sstable; lower values improve scan performance but increase write amplification
storage.value_separation.max_reference_d.name: storage.value_separation.max_reference_depth

storage.value_separation.minimum_size.description: the minimum size of a value that will be separated into a blob file
storage.value_separation.minimum_size.name: storage.value_separation.minimum_size

storage.value_separation.rewrite_minimum.description: the minimum age of a blob file before it is eligible for a rewrite compaction
storage.value_separation.rewrite_minimum.name: storage.value_separation.rewrite_minimum_age

storage.wal_failover.unhealthy_op_thresh.description: the latency of a WAL write considered unhealthy and triggers a failover to a secondary WAL location
storage.wal_failover.unhealthy_op_thresh.name: storage.wal_failover.unhealthy_op_threshold

stream_replication.consumer_heartbeat_fr.description: controls frequency the stream replication destination cluster sends heartbeat to the source cluster to keep the stream alive
stream_replication.consumer_heartbeat_fr.name: physical_replication.consumer.heartbeat_frequency

stream_replication.job_checkpoint_freque.description: controls the frequency with which partitions update their progress; if 0, disabled
stream_replication.job_checkpoint_freque.name: physical_replication.consumer.job_checkpoint_frequency

stream_replication.lag_check_frequency.description: frequency at which the consumer job checks for lagging nodes
stream_replication.lag_check_frequency.name: stream_replication.lag_check_frequency

stream_replication.min_checkpoint_freque.description: controls minimum frequency the stream replication source cluster sends checkpoints to the destination cluster
stream_replication.min_checkpoint_freque.name: physical_replication.producer.min_checkpoint_frequency

stream_replication.replan_flow_frequency.description: frequency at which the consumer job checks to refresh its physical execution plan
stream_replication.replan_flow_frequency.name: physical_replication.consumer.replan_flow_frequency

stream_replication.replan_flow_threshold.description: fraction of nodes in the producer or consumer job that would need to change to refresh the physical execution plan. If set to 0, the physical plan will not automatically refresh.
stream_replication.replan_flow_threshold.name: physical_replication.consumer.replan_flow_threshold

stream_replication.stream_liveness_track.description: controls how frequent we check for the liveness of a replication stream producer job
stream_replication.stream_liveness_track.name: physical_replication.producer.stream_liveness_track_frequency

tenant_cost_control.cpu_usage_allowance.description: this much CPU usage per second is considered background usage and doesn't contribute to consumption; for example, if it is set to 10ms, that corresponds to 1% of a CPU
tenant_cost_control.cpu_usage_allowance.name: tenant_cost_control.cpu_usage_allowance

tenant_cost_control.initial_request.description: number of tokens to get from server on first request (requires restart)
tenant_cost_control.initial_request.name: tenant_cost_control.initial_request

tenant_cost_control.provisioned_vcpus.description: number of vcpus available to the virtual cluster
tenant_cost_control.provisioned_vcpus.name: tenant_cost_control.provisioned_vcpus

tenant_cost_control.token_request_period.description: target duration between token bucket requests (requires restart)
tenant_cost_control.token_request_period.name: tenant_cost_control.token_request_period

tenant_cost_model.cross_region_network_c.description: network cost table for cross-region traffic
tenant_cost_model.cross_region_network_c.name: tenant_cost_model.cross_region_network_cost

tenant_cost_model.estimated_cpu.description: parameters for KV CPU prediction model, in json format
tenant_cost_model.estimated_cpu.name: tenant_cost_model.estimated_cpu

tenant_cost_model.external_io_egress_per.description: cost of a write to external storage in Request Units per MiB
tenant_cost_model.external_io_egress_per.name: tenant_cost_model.external_io_egress_per_mebibyte

tenant_cost_model.external_io_ingress_pe.name: tenant_cost_model.external_io_ingress_per_mebibyte

tenant_cost_model.pgwire_egress_cost_per.description: cost of client <-> SQL ingress/egress per MiB
tenant_cost_model.pgwire_egress_cost_per.name: tenant_cost_model.pgwire_egress_cost_per_mebibyte

tenant_cost_model.read_batch_cost.description: base cost of a read batch in Request Units
tenant_cost_model.read_batch_cost.name: tenant_cost_model.read_batch_cost

tenant_cost_model.read_payload_cost_per_.description: cost of a read payload in Request Units per MiB
tenant_cost_model.read_payload_cost_per_.name: tenant_cost_model.read_payload_cost_per_mebibyte

tenant_cost_model.read_request_cost.description: base cost of a read request in Request Units
tenant_cost_model.read_request_cost.name: tenant_cost_model.read_request_cost

tenant_cost_model.sql_cpu_second_cost.description: cost of a CPU-second in SQL pods in Request Units
tenant_cost_model.sql_cpu_second_cost.name: tenant_cost_model.sql_cpu_second_cost

tenant_cost_model.write_batch_cost.description: base cost of a write batch in Request Units
tenant_cost_model.write_batch_cost.name: tenant_cost_model.write_batch_cost

tenant_cost_model.write_payload_cost_per.description: cost of a write payload in Request Units per MiB
tenant_cost_model.write_payload_cost_per.name: tenant_cost_model.write_payload_cost_per_mebibyte

tenant_cost_model.write_request_cost.description: base cost of a write request in Request Units
tenant_cost_model.write_request_cost.name: tenant_cost_model.write_request_cost

tenant_external_io_default_bytes_allowed.description: controls how many bytes will be read/written before blocking for RUs when writing to external storage
tenant_external_io_default_bytes_allowed.name: tenant_cost_control.external_io.byte_usage_allowance

tenant_global_metrics_exporter_interval.description: the interval at which a node in the cluster will update the exported global metrics
tenant_global_metrics_exporter_interval.name: tenant_global_metrics_exporter_interval

tenant_usage_instance_inactivity.description: server instances that have not reported consumption for longer than this value are cleaned up; should be at least four times higher than the tenant_cost_control.token_request_period of any tenant
tenant_usage_instance_inactivity.name: tenant_cost_control.instance_inactivity.timeout

timeseries.storage.enabled.description: if set, periodic timeseries data is stored within the cluster; disabling is not recommended unless you are storing the data elsewhere
timeseries.storage.enabled.name: timeseries.storage.enabled

timeseries.storage.resolution_10s.ttl.description: the maximum age of time series data stored at the 10 second resolution. Data older than this is subject to rollup and deletion.
timeseries.storage.resolution_10s.ttl.name: timeseries.storage.resolution_10s.ttl

timeseries.storage.resolution_30m.ttl.description: the maximum age of time series data stored at the 30 minute resolution. Data older than this is subject to deletion.
timeseries.storage.resolution_30m.ttl.name: timeseries.storage.resolution_30m.ttl

trace.debug.enable.description: if set, traces for recent requests can be seen at https://<ui>/debug/requests
trace.debug.enable.name: trace.debug_http_endpoint.enabled

trace.opentelemetry.collector.description: address of an OpenTelemetry trace collector to receive traces using the otel gRPC protocol, as <host>:<port>. If no port is specified, 4317 will be used.
trace.opentelemetry.collector.name: trace.opentelemetry.collector

trace.redactable.enabled.description: set to true to enable finer-grainer redactability for unstructured events in traces
trace.redactable.enabled.name: trace.redactable.enabled

trace.snapshot.rate.description: if non-zero, interval at which background trace snapshots are captured
trace.snapshot.rate.name: trace.snapshot.rate

trace.span.force_verbose_regexp.description: a regular expression representing trace span operation names that should be forced into a verbose recording mode. If a span is created whose operation name matches the provided regular expression, the span and all of its children are forced into a verbose recording mode, meaning that the spans will capture all structured logs and events recorded during the lifetime of the span(s). NOTE: the verbose recording mode is known to cause non-trivial amounts of overhead. Using this setting could have a negative impact on cluster latency.
trace.span.force_verbose_regexp.name: trace.span.force_verbose_regexp

trace.span_registry.enabled.description: if set, ongoing traces can be seen at https://<ui>/#/debug/tracez
trace.span_registry.enabled.name: trace.span_registry.enabled

trace.zipkin.collector.description: the address of a Zipkin instance to receive traces, as <host>:<port>. If no port is specified, 9411 will be used.
trace.zipkin.collector.name: trace.zipkin.collector

ui.database_locality_metadata.enabled.description: if enabled shows extended locality data about databases and tables in DB Console which can be expensive to compute
ui.database_locality_metadata.enabled.name: ui.database_locality_metadata.enabled

ui.default_timezone.description: the default timezone used to format timestamps in the ui
ui.default_timezone.name: ui.default_timezone

ui.display_timezone.description: the timezone used to format timestamps in the ui. This setting is deprecatedand will be removed in a future version. Use the 'ui.default_timezone' setting instead. 'ui.default_timezone' takes precedence over this setting. [etc/utc = 0, america/new_york = 1]
ui.display_timezone.name: ui.display_timezone

version.description: set the active cluster version in the format '<major>.<minor>'
version.name: version
